sections.content: RFC-005 resolves canonical relation type cardinality by keeping canonical SRS relation types broad by default. `many-to-many` is the default unless a relation's meaning intrinsically requires stricter cardinality. `supersedes` remains `many-to-many` in the canonical SRS relation vocabulary. Domain packages may publish stricter relation types when their policy requires narrower cardinality, lifecycle preconditions, or source/target constraints.
sections.label: Decision
sections.name: decision

sections.content: Broad canonical cardinality keeps the base vocabulary reusable across spec, governance, provenance, and document-composition contexts. A single record may supersede several older records, several records may supersede different parts of one earlier record, and a consolidated record may supersede a cluster of prior material. Stricter supersession semantics are governance policy, so they belong in installed domain relation packages rather than in the canonical SRS relation vocabulary.
sections.label: Rationale
sections.name: rationale
